title: Fix actor initialization and deposit network connectivity errors

requirements.text: Fix the 'Actor not available' error in WalletDeposit.tsx by ensuring the backend actor is fully initialized before any deposit operations. Add null checks and loading states to prevent premature access to the actor instance.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Actor not available' error no longer appears when users access the deposit page
Actor initialization is verified before rendering deposit form components
Loading state is displayed while actor initializes
User-friendly error message is shown if actor fails to initialize

requirements.text: Resolve deposit network connectivity issues in WalletDeposit.tsx by improving the actor connection sequence. Ensure canister ID resolution, authentication state verification, and actor creation all complete successfully before allowing deposit submission.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can successfully submit deposit requests without network errors
Connection state is properly validated before deposit operations
Network failures trigger automatic retry logic with exponential backoff
Clear error messages guide users when network issues occur

requirements.text: Add defensive checks in the deposit submission flow to verify the actor instance exists and is ready before calling backend methods. Wrap all actor method calls in try-catch blocks with specific error handling for actor availability issues.
acceptanceCriteria: All backend actor method calls are protected with existence checks
Try-catch blocks capture and handle actor-related errors gracefully
Users see specific error messages for different failure scenarios
Failed operations can be retried without refreshing the page

requirements.text: Enhance the useActor hook to ensure it returns a stable, initialized actor instance with proper error state management. Add validation to confirm the actor is ready before returning it to consuming components.
acceptanceCriteria: useActor hook validates actor readiness before returning instance
Hook provides clear error states when actor initialization fails
Consuming components can reliably detect when actor is unavailable
Actor instance remains stable across component re-renders

requirements.text: Add comprehensive error logging in WalletDeposit.tsx to capture the full actor initialization and deposit submission flow. Log actor creation status, canister ID resolution, authentication state, connection attempts, and all thrown exceptions to diagnose network issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs capture all critical steps in the actor initialization sequence
Error logs include stack traces and relevant state information
Network request failures are logged with response details
Logs help identify whether issues are authentication, network, or actor-related

constraints: Do not modify the backend/main.mo actor implementation
Do not change the authentication flow or Internet Identity integration
Preserve all existing error retry logic with exponential backoff
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx
nonGoals: Changing the UPI payment flow or displayed UPI ID
Adding new features beyond fixing the actor and network errors
Changing the admin panel authentication or password
